entries.claim: The agent CredentialStore round-trips all typed secret classes and rejects empty secrets, wrong-class copies, metadata mutation, delimiter collisions, and plaintext-at-rest regressions.
entries.evidence: The secrets test run passed the typed class round-trip, empty-secret rejection, wrong-class copy, envelope class/id/version mutation, delimiter-safe AAD, ciphertext sentinel, owner-only file, wrong-key redaction, and delete/local-key persistence tests.

entries.claim: The credential resolver and provider seams centralize key-source precedence and provider credential id construction without leaking credential material.
entries.command: cargo +1.95.0 test -p isyncyou-agent credential -- --nocapture && cargo +1.95.0 test -p isyncyou-agent provider_ -- --nocapture
entries.evidence: Resolver/provider tests passed for provided/process/env/local key precedence, owner-only local key generation, redacted resolver errors, stable provider secret ids, rejected unsafe ids, and store-backed API-key lookup.

entries.claim: App-host subscription and Codex credential consumers use the central agent credential store helper instead of repeated LocalKey construction.
entries.evidence: App-host credential tests passed for subscription and Codex round-trips, credential-store precedence over desktop CLI fallback, and redacted stored credential errors.

entries.claim: The default mobile Rust path requires an installed agent credential key before opening local data, and the self-test feature validates the process-installed key path.
entries.evidence: Mobile tests passed for key length validation, panic/failure handling, successful process key install, fail-closed start_inner behavior without the key, and feature-gated self-test JSON. Android does not use a default encrypted-file fallback for this key path; if the Keystore/JNI install is unavailable, startup fails closed before local data opens.

entries.claim: Pixel 8 Pro instrumentation proved a separate Android Keystore-wrapped agent credential key installs through JNI and seals a provider credential store without plaintext sentinel residue.
entries.evidence: Instrumentation rerun on Pixel 8 Pro at b50bd14 reported tests=1 failures=0 errors=0 skipped=0. Extracted JSON shows alias=isyncyou-agent-credential-wrap-v1, key_file=agent_credential.key, inside_secure_hardware=true, security_level=trusted_environment, key_source=android_installed, round_trip=true, and no plaintext sentinel in the credential store or wrapped key file.

entries.claim: The #620 branch is green under the targeted host gates for formatting, clippy, Android JVM tests, traceability, evidence validation, and whitespace checks.
entries.command: cargo +1.95.0 fmt --all --check && cargo +1.95.0 clippy -p isyncyou-agent --all-targets --features http,agent-subscription-experimental -- -D warnings && cargo +1.95.0 clippy -p isyncyou-app-host --all-targets --features agent-subscription-experimental -- -D warnings && cargo +1.95.0 clippy -p isyncyou-mobile --all-targets --features agent-credential-store-self-test -- -D warnings && (cd android && ./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest) && python3 tools/check_traceability.py && python3 tools/check_evidence.py --manifest docs/evidence/issue-620-manifest.json && git diff --check origin/dev...HEAD
entries.evidence: Targeted host gates passed during Task 1 through Task 7; final plan-audit gates are rerun before push/PR.
